trg-international.title: TRG International 👍
trg-international.subtitle: first ever internship
trg-international.description:
- i joined the HR team to learn soft skills, but i utilized my coding skills to create apps
- this was back when i didn't know about coding agents...
- i represented the company at career fairs, pulled in 150 applicants!
- i participated in csr activities: tree planting, blood donation, food donation, so funnn
- i direct messaged the ceo and inspired him to start a talk series!

utm-career-center.title: UTM Career Center
utm-career-center.subtitle: first ever paid job
utm-career-center.description:
- i ensured all the technologies are accessible
- not really a tech job but i took initiatives to create coding projects
- first time learning about accessibility, they are actually so important!
- felt good to finally get paid...

go-on-hacks.title: Go On Hacks
go-on-hacks.subtitle: the most weird hackathon
go-on-hacks.description:
- we created a tool that turns every face on your screen into lebron james
- this was so stupid lol, but i learnt a lot from making a browser extension
- i realized smart people to do crazy things with their imagination
- first time in a hackathon i contributed something meaningful!

ember-hacks.title: EmberHacks
ember-hacks.subtitle: organized my first hackathon
ember-hacks.description:
- it was super eye-opening to be on the organizing side
- 100 hackers came, i was in charged of the merch
- as an organizer, i fell asleep before the hackers
- mlh partnered with us and they brought amazing prizes

university-of-toronto.title: University of Toronto
university-of-toronto.subtitle: my realization about university
university-of-toronto.description: i study comp sci at the uoft mississauga, but i usually call myself uoft (shhh no one knows), i am trying my very best to hang on to a 3.58 gpa, i realized that the most important thing from university is not the knowledge but the friends and networking you grow along the way!

techto-hackathon.title: TechTO Hackathon
techto-hackathon.subtitle: the quickest hackathon ever
techto-hackathon.description:
- we had 6 hours to create a functioning app
- i didn't know how to code back then so i helped my team edit videos, and set up some environments
- there was a 16yo in my team, super cracked!

hack-the-north.title: Hack The North
hack-the-north.subtitle: canada's biggest hackathon
hack-the-north.description:
- i stayed for 2 nights at this hackathon (first time overnight)
- competed with over 1200+ hackers, and witnessed a world record being broken!
- i literally got sick after this
- i learnt from my teammate how to use cursor for the first time ever

ignition-hacks.title: Ignition Hacks
ignition-hacks.subtitle: first ever workshop
ignition-hacks.description:
- i hosted my first ever workshop for 100 people
- well only 5 came, i was super shy
- for the first time i was really inexperienced and i felt left out
- i left the event early, haiz, not looking back it was a learning experience!

aws-summit.title: AWS Summit
aws-summit.subtitle: aws largest tech event
aws-summit.description:
- i arrived here at 7am and stayed until 5pm
- with over 10,000 attendees, i learned so much information from industry experts
- i got a chance to train my own ai model, with a chance to win a trip to vegas (i didn't win)

google-devfest.title: Google DevFest
google-devfest.subtitle: huge google workshop
google-devfest.description:
- i participated in a Gemini workshop with 500+ guests
- stood up from the crowd to ask questions (literally just stood up to overcome my fear, i did it!)

murder-mystery-party.title: Murder Mystery Party 👍
murder-mystery-party.subtitle: party for founders and tech people
murder-mystery-party.description:
- i was 4,500km from home, but i attended a Halloween party for young entrepreneurs
- i wore a mask the entire night (completely anonymous)
- met really interesting people, some i ended up working on projects with now back in toronto!

shut-up-and-code.title: Shut Up & Code
shut-up-and-code.subtitle: meeting strangers to code
shut-up-and-code.description:
- strangers just met up to code and learn from each other
- i was a bit shy at first, but i ended up meeting some amazing people there

wallyhacks.title: WallyHacks
wallyhacks.subtitle: first ever hackathon first ever win
wallyhacks.description:
- first ever hackathon i've been to
- it was hardware and i know nothing
- a 4th year student carried us to second place
- i learnt so much, i helped with logics and cleaned up the cables!

codequest.title: CodeQuest
codequest.subtitle: leetcode competition
codequest.description:
- i was speedrunning through the questions and got first place!
- first ever coding competition, it was all fun tho

bell-sap-case-competition.subtitle: first ever case competition
bell-sap-case-competition.description: due to traffic, i booked a $50 uber from toronto to mississauga, arrived 40 minutes late, but i still attend, and the company representatives notice my energy, they invited me to a private coffee chat after the event!

deltahacks.subtitle: 1st hackathon in the streak
deltahacks.description:
- didn't win but this is one of my favourite projects ever
- we analyzed flight paths of 3m+ different flights to see how much fuels we can save if planes fly in birds formation
- so cool to see how nature works

amazon-fulfillment-center.subtitle: 2 hour trip opened my eyes
amazon-fulfillment-center.description:
- it was super cool to see the most advanced robotics being used here
- we weren't allowed to film or record anything but it was super cool
-

frasershacks-2026.title: FrasersHacks
frasershacks-2026.subtitle: first time judging
frasershacks-2026.description:
- a high school student reached out to me to be a judge
- super surprised because it was my first time judging
- funny because my friend was invited as well, so we judged together

deerhacks-v.title: DeerHacks
deerhacks-v.subtitle: first solo hackathon
deerhacks-v.description:
- i solo'ed the hackathon because i had a comedy show that night
- i went to tech roast (the famous guys on tiktok, it was super fun)
- never felt more alone, happy with my project but will never solo again...

ai-agents-waterloo-voice-hackathon.title: AI Agents Voice Hackathon
ai-agents-waterloo-voice-hackathon.subtitle: 6th hackathon in the streak
ai-agents-waterloo-voice-hackathon.description:
- met up with a vc (yes, a vc doing a hackathon) in downtown to work on this hackathon online
- i contributed a lot at first but it was valentines
- yes i dipped the hackathon to go out for valentines and came back after (we did not win)

hack-canada-2026.subtitle: 9th hackathon in the streak
hack-canada-2026.description:
- we didn't have table and chair for this hackathon, rooms were banned
- i sneaked into rooms, avoided organizers, was super fun
- during the hackathon, i commuted 2 hours to another hackathon for the opening ceremony and came back
- we won though!

uofthacks-13.title: UoftHacks
uofthacks-13.subtitle: 2nd hackathon in the streak
uofthacks-13.description:
- i heard half the people got poisoned omg (it was pasta, it was super good tho, i had two portions and i was fine)
- super fun hackathon, managed to make friends with a lot of people
- i learnt that hackathons are the best way for connections
- we won a follow-up award

utra-hacks-2026.title: UTRA Hacks
utra-hacks-2026.subtitle: 4th hackathon in the streak
utra-hacks-2026.description:
- i hated hardware, and this was a hardware hackathon
- i touched hardware a bit, helped assemble the car
- i solo'ed the software part for the team, and we ended up winning the software prize!

qhacks.title: QHacks 👍
qhacks.subtitle: 5th hackathon in the streak, favourite hackathon
qhacks.description:
- we spent $200 going back and forth to kingston
- during the hackathon, we recruited organizers to be our agents, we asked them to go scout other projects, and give us extra food
- i landed a bottle flip trick shot on camera
- a hackathon where i had a bed to sleep on!
- our project was impressive and had a lot of features, judges gave us extra time to demo

kingston-mayors-innovation-challenge.subtitle: i met the mayor of kingston
kingston-mayors-innovation-challenge.description:
- following up qhacks, we got selected to pitch the mayor at city hall
- spend another $200 for the trip, we didn't win anything
- but our project was the most technically complex one!

hack-the-globe.subtitle: 12th hackathon in the streak
hack-the-globe.description:
- it was a combined case comp and hackathon
- we realized too late that ours are too complex and most of them focused on the pitching
- but we stayed up on the streets until 4am because the venue was closed

stupid-ideas-hackathon.title: Stupid Ideas Hackathon
stupid-ideas-hackathon.subtitle: 14th hackathon in the streak
stupid-ideas-hackathon.description:
- we remade google chrome but 2016 version in 2015 version
- included so many easter eggs and games
- we had clash royale, vines, youtube, spotify, chess, bottle flip, etc. etc. all from 2016

smilehacks-2026.subtitle: 7th hackathon in the streak
smilehacks-2026.description:
- i thought my streak would end here, there were no more hackathons to go to until this pops up
- i pretty much carried the entire project, for the first time, since my teammates are non technical
- we got third place, my first ever podium win
- we also won the best video, so two prizes at the same time
- i also made friends with couple of investors who are interested in our project

stan-hackai-toronto.title: Stan x HackAI Hackathon 👍
stan-hackai-toronto.subtitle: 3rd hackathon the streak
stan-hackai-toronto.description:
- craziest hackathon we been to so far
- there were salmon, steak, sushi for food
- when we were hungry, they bought us extra food
- the prize was $20,000 for the first place
- me and my friend sneaked out and bought soju in the middle of the night

hack-the-future.title: Hack The Future 👍
hack-the-future.subtitle: 10th hackathon in the streak
hack-the-future.description:
- first time i ever win first place
- and it was at google office
- we had to go to 3 different rounds to get here
- this was so crazy, i messed up my demo a bit but we still won
- the most proud moment ever, my team had bbq afterwards

sandcastle-sandbox.title: sandcastle
sandcastle-sandbox.subtitle: hackathon at a cafe
sandcastle-sandbox.description:
- the organizers got inspired from my hackathon to create their own
- love the prizes, they were giving out pokemon cards
- it was at a cafe shop, there was place to lie down and sleep too

nvidia-spark-hack-toronto.title: NVIDIA Spark Hack
nvidia-spark-hack-toronto.subtitle: official hackathon by nvidia
nvidia-spark-hack-toronto.description:
- there were too much fun things to do we slacked off
- i pulled off a sick project the night before submission, it was great!
- we got distracted by the indoor basketball, ping pong, pool, arcade machine, etc. (it was super fun tho)
- played board games, catan, while working on the project
- the prize was a $6,000 computer

soon-hackathon.title: SOON Hackathon 👍
soon-hackathon.subtitle: my very own hackathon
soon-hackathon.description:
- creating SOON, i wanted this to the most memorable hackathon ever
- i promised companies branding, solutions, connections, new hires
- i promised hackers food, networking, memories
- we created Something Out Of Nothing
- i gave my team 2 months to plan this entire hackathon from scratch
- we truly created something special, and will continue to do so

genai-genesis-2026.title: GenAI Genesis
genai-genesis-2026.subtitle: 11th hackathon in the streak
genai-genesis-2026.description:
- i organized the hackathon for 1000+ people
- a lot of running arounds, a lot of work
- in the middle, i ran back to mississauga to help with a case competition and came back
- to host a hackathon at this scale was so fun, i bought korean fried chicken for all the hackers

bearhacks-2025.title: BearHacks
bearhacks-2025.subtitle: judging and leading a workshop
bearhacks-2025.description:
- so many cool projects in this hackathon, impressive hardware projects
- i led the workshop again, last time i had 5 people ca me, this time 70 people came to listen, half of the people there!
- was super fun to be on the other side, managed to connect with other judges
- among all the judges, i was the youngest and dumbest one haha

cutc-2026.title: CUTC Tech Conference
cutc-2026.subtitle: got recognized in public
cutc-2026.description:
- after soon hackathon, coming to a major event like this, i am so surprised i got recognized, and some asked to take pictures with me
- it was so fun to meet other people here, people loved what i do
- talked to many founders, recruiters, and ceos, made them remember who i am!

homecoming-ttw.title: Homecoming
homecoming-ttw.subtitle: biggest tech talk ever
homecoming-ttw.description:
- thousands of people gather here to listen to top ceos share their stories
- i made very meaningful connections here, talked to a lot of people
- i skipped work to come here, but my eyes always looked over the slack messages
- let my claude code run while i listened to the talk

cohere-party.title: Cohere Party 👍
cohere-party.subtitle: craziest party with craziest people
cohere-party.description:
- met the mayor of toronto at this party
- and also cohere founders
- needed to crack a riddle and beat an ai system to get the invitation ticket
- met so many new and cool people, they are all super cracked and ambitious
- for the first time i felt like i was in sf!

ai-collective-ontario-hackathons-mixer.title: Ontario Hackathons Mixer
ai-collective-ontario-hackathons-mixer.subtitle: meetups for hackathon organizers
ai-collective-ontario-hackathons-mixer.description: - super niche meetup for hackathon enthusiasts

google-office-tour.subtitle: my favourite office yet
google-office-tour.description:
- skipped my midterm to go to this office tour
- absolutely loved the interior, it was very well designed
- met very cool people here
- this fueled my google dream!

microsoft-canada-hq.subtitle: the first office tour
microsoft-canada-hq.description:
- first time i visit a big company's office
- the view was amazing, right next to cn tower
- this made me wanting to work for these companies, so inspiring

shopify-office-tour.subtitle: super creative office tour
shopify-office-tour.description:
- they had indoor karaoke, ping pong, and golf
- i love this office so much, the food were free, the snacks were unlimited
- listened to the head of engineer share his stories, love to hear how someone started their tech journey
- met friends here who worked at shopify!

deloitte-office-tour.subtitle: a very interesting office tour
deloitte-office-tour.description:
- just the vibes, i don't know, the food was good, but the vibes make me feel like i don't like consulting that much
- office was very beautiful but empty at the same time
- it was a very interesting office tour

aucctus-ai-internship.title: Aucctus AI 👍
aucctus-ai-internship.subtitle: first every tech internship
aucctus-ai-internship.description:
- where everything begun, it was during my finals when i did their take home project
- sacrificed my grades for this internship, it was super worth it
- super fun to work at a startup, everything moved very fast, and we were working with f500 companies
- i owned features, made a new pipeline from scratch and impressed the ceo, and managed to get it out to clients
- for the first time i loved my job!
